forecasts.reasoning: During his current term, Trump engaged with Zelenskyy on Feb 13 (phone), Feb 28 (Oval Office, famously heated), and March 19 (phone, 1 hour). This shows they are willing to communicate frequently over the phone when actively negotiating, even after a strained in-person meeting. With almost 8 weeks having passed since their last confirmed call on March 19, they are arguably 'due' for another check-in, especially with the expiration of the 30-day partial ceasefire they negotiated. Furthermore, the G7 Summit in Kananaskis is scheduled to begin on June 15, 2025. Given that Ukraine will be a central topic, there is a strong mechanistic incentive for the two leaders to speak on the phone to coordinate ahead of the summit. The resolution window perfectly captures the 4-week lead-up to the G7. However, given their occasionally strained relationship, Trump may delegate interactions or they may simply wait until the summit itself to talk, capping the probability. I estimate a 58% chance they speak during this 33-day window.
